environment_policy: scrub env to PATH/system temp only; HOME and package caches are temp; no token/key/secret/cookie/session variables
temp_copy_policy: copy source to TemporaryDirectory; exclude .git, node_modules, .venv, __pycache__, .DS_Store, AppleDouble files; materialize selected public git paths

cases.distilled_lesson: Browser-agent repos need a split between local deterministic tests and browser/network/cloud tests; ShipGrade should preserve that split in Chinese templates.

argv:
from pathlib import Path
root = Path('.')
skills = sorted(root.glob('skills/*/SKILL.md'))
claude = sorted(root.glob('.claude/commands/*.md'))
gemini = sorted(root.glob('.gemini/commands/*.toml'))
docs = [root / 'docs/skill-anatomy.md', root / 'docs/getting-started.md', root / 'docs/cursor-setup.md']
agents = sorted(root.glob('agents/*.md'))
missing_docs = [str(path) for path in docs if not path.exists()]
assert len(skills) >= 20, len(skills)
assert len(claude) >= 6, len(claude)
assert len(gemini) >= 6, len(gemini)
assert len(agents) >= 3, len(agents)
assert not missing_docs, missing_docs
print(f'agent-skills-structure-ok skills={len(skills)} claude={len(claude)} gemini={len(gemini)} agents={len(agents)}')

cases.distilled_lesson: A world-class skill pack should prove cross-tool command parity and hook behavior with tiny local tests, not just present polished skill prose.
